channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the Fonto co for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hawley makes push to allow parents to sue tech companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) said Tuesday he will introduce legislation to empower parents to sue leading social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Did extreme overclockers inspire Microsoft to develop closed loop liquid cooling for data centers? I'd like to think so
item.description: Microsoft is switching to closed-loop liquid cooling, as used by PC overclockers, for its data centers to eliminate water wastage.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Sony needs a bit of gentle encouragement with the PlayStation 5, and this rumored Steam console from Valve might be the perfect answer for that
item.description: If the Steam Deck was anything to go by, Valve has a very good shot at competing in the home gaming market - if rumors of a Steam console are true.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly available pornography app available in the European Union, and warned that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple’s new Invites app gives iCloud+ subscribers an easier way to organize parties – and Android fans are invited too
item.description: Apple just announced an entirely new app, Apple Invites, and it's all about event planning. Here's everything you need to know, including why you need iCloud+.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Bill Gates: Musk wouldn't be telling USAID workers to say home if he knew what it does
item.description: Billionaire Bill Gates said in an interview early Tuesday that Elon Musk "wouldn't be telling" employees at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) not to come to work if he "really knew" what it does. “I give billions of dollars to the same thing that USAID does.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Linux containers in 2025 and beyond
item.description: The use of Linux containers has shown no sign of slowing down since their emergence in the early 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CISA Hires Former DHS CIO in Top Cyber Position - Federal News Network
item.description: CISA hires former DHS CIO Karen Evans to senior advisor for cybersecurity position, sparking speculation about potential permanent role or move to top position at DHS headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: Why second-user smartphone sales are good for Apple
item.description: Apple’s iPhone business isn’t just about new product sales. The second-user and services markets need to be considered, too.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU supports AI challenge to Silicon Valley and China
item.description: The European Union is backing a major AI initiative, OpenEuroLLM, in a bid to counter the dominance of Silicon Valley and China in artificial intelligence. The initiative is being led by Jan Hajič, a computational linguist at Charles University in Czechia, and Peter Sarlin, co-founder of Silo AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big rigs deliver cargo with no humans at the wheel
item.description: First driverless semi delivers 100 loads in Texas, transforming logistics with AI. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The DeepSeek lesson
item.description: During the past two weeks, DeepSeek unraveled Silicon Valley’s comfortable narrative about generative artificial intelligence by introducing dramatically more efficient ways to scale large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Clicks iPhone keyboard is as powerful as it is frustrating
item.description: A funny thing happened after I stopped using Clicks, the keyboard case that effectively turns an iPhone into an oversized Blackberry: The phone by itself suddenly seemed punier. I mean that in terms of both size and mightiness.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: China unveils the world’s fastest high-speed train with a top speed of 280 mph
item.description: China recently unveiled a new bullet train prototype with a test speed of 280 mph that is poised to break records and set new standards.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This free music-streaming site can replace your Spotify subscription
item.description: You know what I miss? Listening to the radio. I’ve always loved background music, which helps me focus. But modern music-streaming services can be distracting. Yes, I enjoy having instant access to millions of songs with services like Spotify.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Wheeled wonder robot dog shows off crazy dance moves in all kinds of tough terrain
item.description: Lynx the robot dog from China is pushing boundaries of what autonomous mobile robots can achieve with a groundbreaking design and unique mobility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sourcegraph unveils AI coding agents
item.description: Looking to challenge well-established GitHub Copilot in AI-powered coding assistance, Sourcegraph has announced coding agents for code review, code migration, testing, documentation, and notification, along with an API for building custom agents. Sourcegraph made the announcement on January 29.
item.dc:creator: Info World
